catalog_policy: Official-source facts and URLs with original RightOut notes only. No third-party broker-list records or prose.

brokers.name: California DROP
brokers.notes: Eligibility-specific government platform. The public runner never files a request. A future integration must verify residency and use a human-only platform approval.

brokers.name: Google Results About You

sources.title: Find and remove personal contact info in Google Search results

brokers.notes: This lane concerns Google search-result visibility, not source-broker deletion. The public runner performs no account or provider action.

brokers.name: EU and EEA controller erasure reference
brokers.notes: Legal reference only, not a sendable recipient. Controller identity and domain must come from a future platform-owned verifier that the agent and controller content cannot alter.

brokers.name: Adsquare
brokers.notes: The official privacy center says deletion or opt-out requests require only the Mobile Advertising ID and an email address. RightOut adds country solely to validate EU or EEA eligibility. SMTP acceptance remains submitted; a controller response requires human review.

brokers.name: emetriq
brokers.notes: The official privacy notice directs data-subject rights to datenschutz@emetriq.com. RightOut initially discloses only the subject-controlled contact email and country. Because advertising data may be pseudonymous, the controller may need proportionate follow-up context; RightOut never invents or pre-emptively discloses extra identifiers.

brokers.name: EDAA YourOnlineChoices
brokers.notes: Pan-European one-stop preference control for participating online-advertising companies. It turns tailored advertising on or off through browser storage. It is not a universal data-broker registry and is not evidence of controller-wide erasure.

brokers.name: emetriq browser opt-out
brokers.notes: The official control stops further emetriq interest-profile collection in the current browser and relies on a retained opt-out cookie. Previously linked data can no longer be assigned to that browser ID and is described as automatically deleted after a short period; this is not controller-wide person-level proof.

brokers.name: Criteo EU rights portal
brokers.notes: Criteo distinguishes consent withdrawal, Article 17 erasure, Article 21 objection, and access. The official page routes erasure to a controller form, so RightOut keeps this human-only until a deterministic form contract is independently evidenced.

brokers.name: Zeotap EU rights portal
brokers.notes: Zeotap provides a website portal for Cookie-ID data and a mobile rights app for Ad-ID data. Because the relevant identifier must be obtained in the subject's browser or device, this remains human-only and no cross-identifier completeness is claimed.

brokers.name: TruePeopleSearch
brokers.notes: Discovery remains Brave-index-only. A separate native approval can directly read only an encrypted exact candidate URL after operator terms review; index absence never proves removal, redirects/CAPTCHA fail closed, and any absence result is limited to the known listing set. Removal submission remains human-only.

brokers.name: BeenVerified
brokers.notes: Brave-only discovery remains read-only. A separate allow-once removal tool may send one minimum-disclosure deletion and opt-out email to the official privacy address for an attested California subject. SMTP acceptance is submitted, never confirmed_removed. Separate approvals cover inbox verification, confirmation-link opening, and an exact-URL direct recheck after operator terms review; confirmed_removed is scoped to the known listing set.

brokers.name: Intelius
brokers.notes: Discovery uses the Brave index only. The separately approved browser-form lane sends only the contact email to initiate the official suppression flow; email verification and subject selection remain before any removal can be claimed.

brokers.name: US Search
brokers.notes: Discovery uses the Brave index only. The ownership relationship is sourced independently from the official California registry.

brokers.name: Nuwber
brokers.notes: Discovery uses the Brave index only; absence remains inconclusive.

brokers.name: Whitepages
brokers.notes: Official California registry evidence identifies the privacy entry point; automated publisher access remains disabled.

brokers.name: USPeopleSearch
brokers.notes: The request page is cataloged for human planning only; live discovery remains Brave-only.

brokers.name: CheckPeople
brokers.notes: The request entry point is not automated until its form contract and terms are independently verified.

brokers.name: FreePeopleSearch.com
brokers.notes: Discovery uses the Brave index only; no publisher page is fetched.

brokers.name: PeopleWhiz
brokers.notes: The removal entry point remains human-planned until a safe form contract exists.

brokers.name: PeopleFinders
brokers.notes: Discovery uses the Brave index only; no removal form is automated in this lane.

brokers.name: FastPeopleSearch
brokers.notes: The 2025 California registry identifies the ownership cluster and states that a request on one listed site applies across the listed sites; RightOut still requires later per-site verification.

brokers.name: USA People Search
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in an independently evidenced ownership cluster; publisher pages are not fetched.

brokers.name: Advanced Background Checks
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in the official-registry ownership cluster.

brokers.name: People Search Now
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in the official-registry ownership cluster.

brokers.name: USPhoneBook
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in the official-registry ownership cluster.

brokers.name: SearchPeopleFree
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in the official-registry ownership cluster.

brokers.name: SmartBackgroundChecks
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in the official-registry ownership cluster.

brokers.name: CyberBackgroundChecks
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in the official-registry ownership cluster.

brokers.name: FastBackgroundCheck
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in the official-registry ownership cluster.

brokers.name: ThatsThem
brokers.notes: The official registry identifies Spokeo ownership but explicitly describes a separate opt-out for ThatsThem.

brokers.name: Spokeo
brokers.notes: Automated live scanning is disabled. Spokeo's published consumer terms prohibit automated queries, scraping, crawling, data mining, robots, spiders, and other automatic devices; its robots policy disallows search/profile paths. Any review is manual and out of the plugin live tool.
